state.location: The collapsed entrance of the Ashen Barrow
state.scene: Torchlight gutters against damp stone. A goblin sentry, Snik, bars the only passage deeper, blade already drawn. Slumped in the rubble lies an earlier scout, long dead, a water-stained journal still clutched in one hand — its final legible entry warns that the iron door in the deep chamber yields to no key, only to a spoken word: 'ashfall.' Your party: Brother Aldric, a road-worn cleric, and Wisp, a half-elf mage whose spellbook is nearly spent.
log: [turn 4] combat started round 1, order: ['wisp', 'aldric', 'snik'], first: wisp
[turn 7] Wisp uses pearl_of_power: restore_slot ({'ok': True, 'item': 'Pearl of Power', 'effect': 'restore_slot', 'level': 1, 'slots_remaining': 2})
[turn 7] scene -> ember_chamber (The Ashen Barrow — The Ember Chamber)
[turn 8] combat started round 1, order: ['aldric', 'narl', 'wisp', 'grik'], first: aldric
[turn 14] Wisp uses healing_potion: heal ({'ok': True, 'item': 'Potion of Healing', 'effect': 'heal', 'rolled': 8, 'roll_detail': '2d4+2 -> [4, 2] + 2 = 8', 'healed': 8, 'hp': 16})

transcript.text: search the room

Dust and grit coat your fingers as you pick through the collapsed threshold — but the search yields real rewards. Tucked behind a toppled stone is a **healing potion**, its red liquid still bright, and beside it a **pearl of power** that hums with stored magic against Wisp's palm. The dead scout's journal is the grimmer find: the last legible lines, written in a shaking hand, warn that the iron door at the barrow's heart will not budge for any key — only the whispered word *"ashfall"* will open it.
search for loot

Aldric tucks the healing potion into his belt with a relieved grunt, while Wisp cradles the pearl of power — its faint warmth already seeping into her fingers like a whispered promise of magic restored. The rubble yields nothing more; the collapsed entrance has given up all it has to offer. Behind them, the grey daylight fades; ahead, a stone passage descends into cold darkness toward the barrow's heart.
parley with the goblin

Aldric's voice echoes off the damp stone, steady and measured, but Snik only spits at his boots and hefts a rusted blade — the goblin's patience for diplomacy as thin as a knife's edge and just as sharp.
attack the goblin

Steel scrapes stone as Snik snarls and drops into a fighting crouch, rusted blade leveled at Aldric's throat. The air in the collapsed entrance goes electric — Wisp's fingers crackle with unspent magic, Aldric's mace swings up in a white-knuckled grip, and the narrow passage feels suddenly very small.
cast magic missile

Three bolts of cold blue light streak from Wisp's outstretched fingers, punching into Snik one after another with sharp, sizzling cracks. The goblin staggers back against the stone wall, gasping, his rusted blade clattering — barely upright on trembling legs, only a thread of life keeping him standing.
swing mace

Aldric's mace comes down in a single decisive arc, and Snik folds like wet parchment — crumpling to the cold flagstones with a hollow thud, rusted blade spinning off into the dark. The echo of it rolls down the descending passage and dies somewhere deep in the earth below. Silence floods back in.

Both Aldric and Wisp are unscathed — not a scratch between them — though Wisp burned one of her first-level slots on those magic missiles, leaving her magic reserves a little leaner than she'd like. Snik's body lies twisted near the wall, nothing but rags and rust on him worth noting. The collapsed entrance offers no way back into the grey daylight, and only one path remains: the stone passage sloping downward into cold blackness, toward whatever waits in the heart of the Ashen Barrow. A faint smell of old ash and something metallic drifts up from below. What do you do?
wisp uses the pearl of power and they continue through the door

Wisp closes her fist around the lustrous pearl and it dissolves into a cascade of warm light, the lost magic rushing back through her like a second heartbeat; she rolls her shoulders and follows Aldric down the sloping passage. The cold stone opens without warning into a wide, soot-blackened chamber where two braziers bleed orange embers across the walls — and two goblins are already staring straight at them.
attack the goblins!

Aldric's boots hit the chamber floor at a dead sprint, mace already swinging up as the two goblins scramble to their feet — Grik knocking over a clay pot, Narl baring a mouthful of jagged teeth. The fight erupts in the orange glow of dying embers.
aldric uses his mace against narl

Aldric lunges forward and brings the mace down in a brutal overhead arc, catching Narl square on the shoulder with a sickening crack of bone. The goblin's screech tears through the ember-lit chamber as he stumbles sideways, his sword arm hanging limp and useless — still snarling, still standing, but barely.

Narl spins through the pain, teeth bared in a wild grin, and drives a rusty short sword at Wisp with his good arm — the blade finds a gap in her guard and rakes across her ribs, drawing a hiss of pain and a thin line of red through her robes.
wisp casts chromatic orb at grik

Wisp snarls through the pain in her ribs and hurls a crackling sphere of violet force straight at Grik — it detonates against his chest with a thunderclap that sends him skidding into the far wall, scorched leather smoking and his yellow eyes wide with shock. He slumps against the stone, one clawed hand pressed to the blackened wound, very much still in the fight but rattled to his bones.

Grik lunges from behind the scorched brazier with a wild, desperate slash — but his burned arm betrays him at the last moment, the rusty blade skittering harmlessly off the stone floor a foot wide of Wisp's boots.
aldric casts guiding bolt at narl

Narl's ragged snarl dies in his throat as the holy light punches through him — one convulsive twitch, and then stillness, a thin curl of smoke rising from his chest. Grik's yellow eyes dart from his fallen companion to the glowing symbol in Aldric's hand, and for just a heartbeat something that looks almost like fear flickers across that scarred green face.
wisp casts magic missile at grik

The three darts of force slam into Grik one after another with merciless precision, and the goblin folds like wet parchment, sliding down the scorched wall to crumple beside his fallen companion. The rusty blade clatters from his fingers and spins across the flagstones, coming to rest in the ash — and then there is nothing but silence and the soft orange breath of dying embers.

Aldric stands unhurt, mace still warm in his grip, every spell slot spent but steady on his feet. Wisp is less fortunate — Narl's blade left a real mark across her ribs, and four hit points of blood have soaked into her robes; she has one second-level slot left and nothing else to throw. The two goblins lie twisted on the floor, the brazier gutters low, and the chamber smells of char and iron. Across the room the heavy iron door stands silent and shut, its surface etched with faint, soot-darkened letters — it is the only way forward. A healing potion glints from beneath Narl's overturned pack, half-hidden in the ash. What do you do?
wisp picks up the healing potion

Wisp crouches beside Narl's overturned pack and closes her fingers around the small glass vial, its ruby liquid catching the last guttering light of the brazier. She tucks it away with a careful hand — insurance against whatever waits beyond that iron door.
wisp consuimes the healing_potion

Wisp uncorks the vial and drinks it in one practiced motion, the warm ruby liquid spreading through her chest like sunlight on cold stone — the gash along her ribs knits shut with a soft tingle, and color returns to her pale cheeks. She straightens, feeling whole again, and turns her gaze to the iron door.
the party uses the password to go through the iron door

Under a sky thick with autumn stars, Aldric and Wisp walk the rutted road back toward Thornwall, the dark hump of the Ashen Barrow shrinking behind them until it is nothing but a shadow among shadows on the hill. The village lanterns are still burning — someone left them lit, hoping — and when the gate-warden sees them coming he lets out a shout that wakes the whole street, and doors open, and people pour out into the cold with blankets and bread and questions all tumbling together. The barrow is cold. The goblins are down. The dead who were trapped there can rest at last. Aldric presses his holy symbol between both palms and murmurs a quiet thanks to his god, and Wisp, for once, says nothing at all — only tilts her face up to the stars and smiles the private smile of someone who was not entirely sure she would make it back, and did.
